nameMeanings.name: Rix,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and German: variant of Ricks with -x as a spelling of -cks.
English: in southwestern England sometimes a spelling of Ricks 1, from Old English (West Saxon) rixe ‘rush’.

variants.name: Ricks
meanings: English: variant of Rick , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. See also Rickson .

English: topographic name from Middle English rixe, rexe (Old English rixe, a West Saxon metathesized form of Old English risc, rysc ‘rush’) wash. The surname probably originally denoted someone who lived where rushes grew, or it may be a habitational name from a place so named, such as Rix (Devon).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Rix, Earl of Offord was born in 1450, in Huntingdonshire, England, his father, Robert Rix, was 28 and his mother, Mrs. Robert Rix, was 25. He had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Ann Periam. He died in July 1536, in London, England, at the age of 86.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert Rix, Earl of Offord est né en 1450, à Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert Rix, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Robert Rix, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Ann Periam. Il est décédé en juillet 1536, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 86 ans.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert Rix, Earl of Offord родился в 1450 г. в местности хантингдоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Rix было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Robert Rix, 25. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от Ann Periam. Он умер июль 1536 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 86 лет.
